title: LCL acronym
wikitext:
[]: LCL acronym             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed May 06, 2009 6:13 pm [url]http://wiki.evageeks.org/LCL[/url] wrote: * It is unknown what the initials "LCL" stand for, but we do know what they don't mean. According to the Evangelion: Death and Rebirth theatrical program (special edition): "Incidentally, the widely circulated idea that L.C.L is the abbreviation of "Link Connected Liquid" is incorrect." However, nowhere does it say what it does mean. Why they would go out of their way to say what it doesn't stand for, but not go on to explain what it does stand for, is unknown, and a cause of much fan frustration. Evangelion Chronicle suggests that one of the Ls stands for "Lilith".

[]:             Quote Post by Evayo » Wed May 06, 2009 9:19 pm "Lilith's Creation Liquid" could work, but I've always supported the suggestion that it was "Life Component Liquid" since it makes the most sense and sounds applicable.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Wed May 06, 2009 9:48 pm I think "Life Component Liquid" makes more sense than "Lilith's Creation Liquid" as well. It also sounds less awkward. What extra stuff have you found?

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Wed May 06, 2009 10:51 pm The "L" could also stand for left-handed, since the amino acids that make up life on earth have a left-handed molecular structure. For example, the amino acid glutamine always occurs as L-glutamine in nature, but artificially synthesized glutamine is an even mix of L-glutamine and D-glutamine. So LCL could be "Left-handed C of Life" -- I'm just kidding about the C, but you know that those guys at Gainax have a sense of humor. The fact that life on earth only uses left-handed amino acids is one of those fun facts that Anno might have known about.

[]: Re: LCL acronym             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu May 07, 2009 3:04 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Uhh, has anything been found since Chronicle

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu May 07, 2009 3:15 am There's also AchtunAffen's rather pithy suggestion based on the source.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu May 07, 2009 9:09 am @Reichu If it's not a bother, could you find and post the bit in Chronicle that 'suggests that one of the Ls stands for "Lilith"'

[]:             Quote Post by Evayo » Thu May 07, 2009 9:23 am It's at the top of the page in quotes, unless you mean where it was officially stated.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu May 07, 2009 9:27 am Evayo wrote: It's at the top of the page in quotes, unless you mean where it was officially stated.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu May 07, 2009 10:17 am Mr. Tines wrote: There's also AchtunAffen's rather pithy suggestion based on the source.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu May 07, 2009 2:01 pm Lilith's Creation Liquid or Life Component Liquid, either or is the best bet.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu May 07, 2009 2:29 pm Lame Contrivance Liquid </topic>

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu May 07, 2009 2:57 pm Lilith's Cunt Liquid

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu May 07, 2009 2:58 pm ^ Actually I think that's what AA thought the term stood for.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu May 07, 2009 2:58 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Actually I think that's what AA thought the term stood for.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu May 07, 2009 3:05 pm It stands for Lauren Conrad Liquid. We've already established this.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Thu May 07, 2009 3:46 pm Liquid Cosmic Love

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu May 07, 2009 4:58 pm Can't you guys ever be serious?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu May 07, 2009 5:06 pm SSD, you're a moderator. You're supposed to insult them and threaten them. I'll put the source up when I get home tonight.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu May 07, 2009 5:41 pm Reichu: I was hoping that previous post would get the point across. Thanks for posting the source.
